title: Tests and Breasts

jokes.text: Students laughing hysterically at health education film about 'hair growth in the genital area'
jokes.setup: Serious educational film about puberty mentions genital hair growth
jokes.punchline: Entire class erupts in immature laughter
jokes.explanation: Classic teen reaction to sex ed - earned through authentic character behavior but not particularly original

jokes.text: Student comparing female reproductive diagram to something from outer space
jokes.setup: Health class showing anatomical diagram of female reproductive system
jokes.punchline: Looks like it's from outer space
jokes.explanation: Typical juvenile reaction - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Mr. Fredricks threatening Sam about getting 'the clap' for not paying attention
jokes.setup: Sam disrupting health class
jokes.punchline: Teacher's inappropriate threat about contracting an STD
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate teacher comment that's both funny and uncomfortable - good character work

jokes.text: Student correcting 'it jumped out of his stomach' to 'it jumped out of his chest'
jokes.setup: Discussion about Alien movie scene
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction about anatomical accuracy of fictional movie scene
jokes.explanation: Realistic teen pedantry but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Mr. Fredricks sarcastically calling Sam 'Dr. Love' for disrupting class
jokes.setup: Sam disrupting health education class
jokes.punchline: Teacher sarcastically dubbing him medical expert
jokes.explanation: Effective sarcastic nickname that becomes running gag - economical and character-driven

jokes.text: Sam completely unable to identify ovaries on diagram
jokes.setup: Teacher challenges Sam to identify body parts
jokes.punchline: Sam's complete ignorance exposed publicly
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Sam's humiliation is both funny and sympathetic

jokes.text: Game show buzzer sound effect followed by 'Cervix says no'
jokes.setup: Sam incorrectly identifying cervix
jokes.punchline: Teacher treating anatomy lesson like game show with buzzer sound
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment turning anatomy into game show - highly quotable and original

jokes.text: Sam admitting 'I don't know' when asked to identify vagina
jokes.setup: Teacher asks Sam to identify 'easy one' that 'guys know'
jokes.punchline: Sam's honest admission of total ignorance
jokes.explanation: Sam's honest vulnerability creates both humor and sympathy - well-integrated character moment

jokes.text: 'Dr. Love' callback when Sam returns to seat
jokes.setup: Earlier 'Dr. Love' nickname
jokes.punchline: Students repeating the nickname after Sam's humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Sam's humiliation but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Sam's consolation: 'At least I didn't get head lice'
jokes.setup: Sam's complete humiliation in health class
jokes.punchline: Finding silver lining in absurdly low bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Sam's twisted optimism in face of humiliation is both funny and endearing

jokes.text: Mr. Rosso's excessively casual 'Peace, brother' to student
jokes.setup: Guidance counselor trying to be cool with students
jokes.punchline: Overuse of dated hippie slang
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for try-hard counselor but not laugh-out-loud funny

jokes.text: Mr. Rosso saying 'Rock on, Kirby' with finger gesture
jokes.setup: Counselor continuing to try being cool
jokes.punchline: Another dated expression with presumably awkward gesture
jokes.explanation: Reinforces character but diminishing returns on the same joke

jokes.text: Daniel claiming he doesn't ride the bus when teacher uses bus metaphor
jokes.setup: Teacher explaining math learning with bus metaphor
jokes.punchline: Daniel missing metaphor completely, focusing on literal bus riding
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Daniel's literal thinking reveals both his limitations and authenticity

jokes.text: Jocks laughing at dirty joke that geeks don't understand
jokes.setup: Joke about person with no arms/legs ringing doorbell
jokes.punchline: Social hierarchy revealed through humor comprehension
jokes.explanation: Good setup for social dynamics but the actual humor is in the misunderstanding

jokes.text: Neal declaring 'I don't really like jokes. I don't think they're funny.'
jokes.setup: Geeks trying to understand dirty joke
jokes.punchline: Neal's matter-of-fact rejection of humor as concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character line - Neal's earnest confusion about humor itself is perfectly written

jokes.text: 'WOULD YOU AUTOGRAPH MY GENITALS?' - another jock mocking Sam
jokes.setup: Continuing mockery of Sam's Dr. Love reputation
jokes.punchline: Even cruder request escalating the harassment
jokes.explanation: Less clever escalation - shows persistent bullying but less funny than setup

jokes.text: Daniel saying 'I get these weird urges sometimes' after pouring soda on Kim
jokes.setup: Daniel randomly pouring drink on Kim's shirt
jokes.punchline: Understatement about destructive impulses
jokes.explanation: Great character insight delivered as throwaway line - Daniel's self-awareness about his problems

jokes.text: 'IT'S NOT LIKE I'M A BUS DRIVER.' - Daniel's response about studying math
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggesting Daniel could do math if he tried
jokes.punchline: Reference back to teacher's bus metaphor, dismissing bus drivers
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Daniel was listening, plus casual classism typical for his character

jokes.text: Daniel saying 'It's not like I'm a bus driver' about his intelligence
jokes.setup: Daniel claiming he could do math if he wanted
jokes.punchline: Casual classist put-down of blue-collar work
jokes.explanation: Reveals Daniel's insecurities through casual cruelty - good character work

jokes.text: Harris claiming he 'gets' the dirty joke but won't explain it
jokes.setup: Geeks asking older student to explain joke
jokes.punchline: Harris's transparent fake knowledge and circular logic
jokes.explanation: Good portrayal of teenage posturing and insecurity about sexual knowledge

jokes.text: Harris's terrible pickup line: 'Love is like homework. You got to study if you want to get an A.'
jokes.setup: Harris trying to impress with worldly wisdom
jokes.punchline: Painfully awkward attempt at smooth talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - the kind of line teenagers think sounds cool but is actually terrible

jokes.text: Harold insisting on answering door because 'it's dark out, and you're a girl'
jokes.setup: Daniel arriving to study with Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Father's old-fashioned protectiveness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Harold's dated attitudes - realistic but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: Daniel saying 'Just talking about studying makes me want to take a nap'
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to help Daniel study
jokes.punchline: Daniel's complete aversion to academic work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel line - economical delivery of character trait

jokes.text: Nick saying Sam won't learn anything useful from health textbook
jokes.setup: Sam studying human sexuality textbook
jokes.punchline: Nick's worldly dismissal of book learning about sex
jokes.explanation: Sets up Nick as street-smart alternative to book learning - decent character work

jokes.text: Daniel's 'I'LL BE COOL' when giving up on studying
jokes.setup: Daniel realizing he can't learn all the math before the test
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of inevitable failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel response - fatalistic but delivered with characteristic nonchalance

jokes.text: Bill's argument about Hulk vs. Thing including waiting for Bruce Banner transformation
jokes.setup: Geeks debating superhero fight outcomes
jokes.punchline: Strategic thinking about exploiting Hulk's human form
jokes.explanation: Clever geek logic that shows both intelligence and complete absorption in fantasy

jokes.text: Nick thinking ABBA is from Switzerland instead of Sweden
jokes.setup: Students discussing ABBA performance
jokes.punchline: Nick's confident incorrectness about basic pop culture fact
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Nick's casual confidence despite ignorance

jokes.text: Nick saying 'I think I'm getting a wart on my wrist' during Daniel's crisis
jokes.setup: Daniel panicking about algebra test
jokes.punchline: Nick's complete self-absorption with minor health concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick moment - oblivious to others' problems, focused on trivial personal concerns

jokes.text: Neal's disgusted reaction to pornography: 'Eww! Give me that. Only perverts watch pornos, right?'
jokes.setup: Geeks discovering pornographic film
jokes.punchline: Neal's prudish reaction combined with grabbing it anyway
jokes.explanation: Great character contradiction - Neal's moral objection versus curiosity creates perfect tension

jokes.text: 'I DON'T EVEN HAVE A PROJECTOR.' 'WELL, I DO.' - logistics of porn viewing
jokes.setup: Sam's practical concern about how to watch film
jokes.punchline: Neal matter-of-factly solving the technical problem
jokes.explanation: Funny practical problem-solving - reduces porn viewing to technical logistics

jokes.text: Daniel saying 'I ganked the test' casually
jokes.setup: Lindsay expecting Daniel studied
jokes.punchline: Daniel's casual admission of theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel moment - casual criminality delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Kowchevski calling Daniel a 'loser' with 'bedroom eyes and stringy hair'
jokes.setup: Lindsay defending Daniel to teacher
jokes.punchline: Teacher's brutally honest assessment disguised as advice
jokes.explanation: Harsh but funny character moment - teacher's inappropriate honesty about student

jokes.text: Daniel saying Lindsay's 'like a brain or something' for doing math
jokes.setup: Lindsay solving algebra problems in her head
jokes.punchline: Daniel's awed description of basic intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Daniel's perspective on academic ability

jokes.text: 'HOW'D YOU LIKE TO COME HOME TO THAT EVERY NIGHT?' - geek watching porn
jokes.setup: Geeks finally watching pornographic film
jokes.punchline: Neal treating porn star like potential domestic partner
jokes.explanation: Funny naive response - treating pornography as relationship material rather than fantasy

jokes.text: 'He's in good shape' - Bill commenting on male porn star
jokes.setup: Boys watching pornography for first time
jokes.punchline: Bill's clinical observation about male performer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - missing the point completely with innocent observation

jokes.text: 'That guy's got the best job in the world'
jokes.setup: Teenage boys watching pornography
jokes.punchline: Naive view of porn industry as dream job
jokes.explanation: Typical teenage male reaction - predictable but authentic

jokes.text: 'Hey, you think there's any short porn stars?'
jokes.setup: Boys watching pornography
jokes.punchline: Random practical question about porn industry
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific question that captures teenage tangential thinking

jokes.text: 'Are we gonna go to hell for this? I don't wanna go to hell.'
jokes.setup: After watching pornography
jokes.punchline: Bill's genuine religious anxiety about viewing porn
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - innocent religious guilt about normal teenage behavior

jokes.text: Harold comparing Lindsay to Patty Hearst with 'gun to our head'
jokes.setup: Lindsay accused of cheating
jokes.punchline: Father's dramatic escalation to terrorist comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Harold's paranoid thinking - specific 70s reference adds authenticity

jokes.text: 'I DID CHEAT. I LIED AND I CHEATED, AND THEN I LIED SOME MORE.' Millie: 'YOU'RE FUNNY, LINDSAY.'
jokes.setup: Lindsay finally confessing her sins to former best friend
jokes.punchline: Millie's complete inability to believe Lindsay capable of wrongdoing
jokes.explanation: Great ironic moment - Lindsay's reputation for goodness makes confession unbelievable

jokes.text: Daniel's speech about being labeled Track 3 - 'the dumb kids' - at age 11
jokes.setup: Daniel explaining his academic struggles
jokes.punchline: Heartbreaking revelation about educational system crushing child's spirit
jokes.explanation: Devastating character revelation that explains everything about Daniel - perfectly written and delivered

jokes.text: Sam identified by his Star Trek notebook paper
jokes.setup: Teacher claiming anonymous questions
jokes.punchline: Sam's geek identity immediately revealed by stationery choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail showing how Sam's geekiness is inescapable

jokes.text: Harold saying 'I knew kids like Desario in Vietnam' as character reference
jokes.setup: School meeting about cheating
jokes.punchline: Harold invoking war experience about teenage troublemaker
jokes.explanation: Harold's tendency to relate everything to Vietnam creates absurd comparison

jokes.text: Lindsay's hysterical laughter during disciplinary meeting
jokes.setup: Tense disciplinary meeting with parents present
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's complete nervous breakdown expressed as uncontrollable laughter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy showing Lindsay's psychological breaking point - perfectly timed and performed

jokes.text: Harold insisting 'She's as high as a kite!' about Lindsay's laughter
jokes.setup: Lindsay's nervous laughter
jokes.punchline: Harold's immediate assumption of drug use
jokes.explanation: Harold's paranoid leap to drug explanation fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Harold declaring 'She's a track one girl!' while Lindsay laughs hysterically
jokes.setup: Daniel's earlier track system explanation
jokes.punchline: Harold using same system to defend daughter during her breakdown
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Harold completely missing the point while defending Lindsay

tmdb_overview: Lindsay encourages Nick to pursue a career in music. Nick continues to practice with his current band, but quickly decides he's better than them. Lindsay encourages Nick to audition for a bigger local band. When things don't go well in the audition, Lindsay is there to comfort Nick. After learning they have to shower in gym class, Sam, Bill, and Neal refuse to do it.
